_comment: First-party Oh The Biomes We've Gone WET-WOODS compat on the AQUATIC family — WATER-FIRST priority per the Q2 per-seed decision in BWGPLAN.md: the water dominates the island and the BWG wet-wood trees are the SECONDARY layer (contrast the Forest family, where the same wet biomes are trees-first). Bands are PREPENDED so they win the first-match over the base #minecraft:is_ocean/#is_river/etc. catch-alls. Inert without BWG (every biomeswevegone: id is unknown → never matches → byte-identical generation).
_comment_water: #64 (v0.186.0): the water feature is a BROAD SHALLOW SWAMP/MARSH, not the old deep round pond — depth 2 with slope:true (shallowing to 1 at the shore) and a raised extent so it spreads across the island instead of sinking a deep well in the middle. Extent stays under 0.7 on every tier so a wooded dry rim survives for the wet-wood trees (see #65). Trees are guaranteed a floor by the engine's forceOneTree clearing (GenerationJob), so a broader marsh no longer starves the island of trees.
_verify: IDS VERIFIED vs Oh-The-Biomes-Weve-Gone-NeoForge-2.6.0.jar (2026-07-01). Biomes cypress_swamplands / bayou / white_mangrove_marshes / rainbow_beach and CONFIGURED features cypress_trees / bayou_trees / white_mangrove_trees / palm_trees all exist (TreeEntry resolves against the configured-feature registry — Lookup.configuredFeature). NOTE: BWG injects palm_trees into vanilla minecraft:beach via a biome_modifier (there is no BWG palm biome), so palm is keyed onto the sandy rainbow_beach biome by design — still inert without BWG.

biome_overrides._verify: verified: biome cypress_swamplands + configured feature cypress_trees
verified: willow wood grows in biome bayou; feature is bayou_trees (a willow_tree random_selector — there is NO biomeswevegone:willow_trees feature)
verified: white_mangrove wood grows in biome white_mangrove_marshes (NOT pale_bog — that is the spirit biome) + configured feature white_mangrove_trees

verified: configured feature palm_trees exists; biome rainbow_beach chosen by design (BWG injects palm into vanilla minecraft:beach via biome_modifier, not into a BWG biome, so a sandy BWG biome hosts it here)
